clinical_trials.title: Study on Drug Interaction of Metformin Tablets and HRS-7535 Tablets in Healthy Human
clinical_trials.summary: This study was a single-center, open, fixed-sequence, self-controlled trial design conducted in healthy subjects.

clinical_trials.title: Switch From Metformin Monotherapy to a Bitherapy With Metformin and Repaglinide
clinical_trials.summary: This study is conducted in Europe. The aim of this observational study is to investigate the efficacy (e.g. glycemic control) and safety (e.g. hypoglycemic episodes) when adding repaglinide to a monotherapy of metformin in type 2 diabetic patients under normal clinical practice

clinical_trials.title: Metformin With Standard Taxotere and Prednisone in the Treatment of Castration Resistant Prostate Cancer
clinical_trials.summary: This study is being conducted to determine the effectiveness of standard 3 weekly treatments using docetaxel and prednisone with metformin in patients with castration-resistant metastatic prostate cancer.

clinical_trials.title: Efficacy and Long-Term Safety of Vildagliptin as Add-on Therapy to Metformin in Patients With Type 2 Diabetes
clinical_trials.summary: This is a 2-3 period parallel group study with an adaptive element in Type 2 diabetes patients receiving vildagliptin as add-on therapy to metformin

clinical_trials.title: Improving Insulin Resistance To Treat Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease: A Pilot Study
clinical_trials.summary: Metformin is being compared to exercise and diet modifications. The researchers are interested in learning if the addition of metformin to lifestyle modifications is more helpful in treating the condition or disorder. Although metformin is FDA approved to treat type 2 diabetes, it is not FDA approved for the treatment of Non-alcoholic fatty liver (NAFLD) and is considered investigational for the purpose of this study.

clinical_trials.title: Drug Interactions From Simultaneous Administration Of Metformin And GSK189075 To Subjects With Type 2 Diabetes
clinical_trials.summary: GSK189075 is intended for use as a single treatment or in combination with other treatments for tye 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM). Metformin is widely used in Europe and the USA for the treatment of T2DM. This study will evaluate the effect of GSK189075 on metformin levels in the blood in T2DM subjects. The rationale is to look for any safety problems that may result when the 2 drugs are given together.

clinical_trials.title: Bioequivalence Study of Metformin Hydrochloride Extended-Release Tablets USP 750 mg Under Fasting Condition
clinical_trials.summary: The study was a randomized, open label, two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence, single dose, crossover, oral bioequivalence study in normal, healthy adult human subjects under fasting condition.

clinical_trials.title: Study on Drug Interaction of HRS-1780 Tablets, Henagliflozin Tablets, Metformin Tablets and HRS-7535 Tablets in Healthy Human
clinical_trials.summary: This study was designed as a single-center, single-arm, open, fixed-sequence, self-controlled trial in healthy subjects.

clinical_trials.title: Metformin for Chemoprevention of Lung Cancer in Overweight or Obese Individuals at High Risk for Lung Cancer
clinical_trials.summary: This phase II trial determines the effect of metformin extended release on the risk for developing lung cancer in overweight/obese patients that are at high-risk for developing lung cancer. Metformin is widely used to treat type II diabetes and has a long history of safety and minimal side effects. At similar dosage, the drug may have potential anti-cancer activity. Metformin use has been associated with improved survival in patients with non-small cell lung carcinoma, a specific type of lung cancer, and it has also been shown to enhance immune mobilization against tumors. This trial aims to see whether metformin extended release as a preventative treatment may lower the chance of developing lung cancer, and whether it may help patients' immune system learn ("reprogram") to lower a certain type of immune cell (called regulatory T cells) that are linked to tumor development.

clinical_trials.title: Metformin for Treatment of Psoriasis Combined With Disorders of Glucose and Lipid Metabolism
clinical_trials.summary: Psoriasis and metabolic disorders are well-known mutual comorbidities. The investigators hypothesized metformin can ameliorate both psoriasis and metabolic disorders mainly via gut microbiota modulation. The investigators design a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study to evaluate the efficacy and safety of treatment of metformin for psoriasis combined with disorders of glucose and lipid metabolism and to explore the role of gut microbiota during the process.

detailed_analysis.market_summary: diabetes shows relevant disease-burden and health-system signals in the retrieved data. Healthcare expenditure indicators from WHO and World Bank suggest where payer capacity may support new therapies, while any disease-specific prevalence rows highlight the potential patient pool. Taken together, these signals can be used as an early proxy for treatment market attractiveness.

top_opportunities.rationale: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) combines literature, market signals with 2 missing domain(s).
Diabetes Mellitus, Type 2 combines clinical, market signals with 2 missing domain(s).
metabolic disorders combines literature signals with 3 missing domain(s).
Fasting combines clinical signals with 3 missing domain(s).
Lung Carcinoma combines clinical signals with 3 missing domain(s).

llm_report.executive_summary: Metformin is a high-market-potential biguanide primarily indicated for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. Beyond its established role in glycemic control, clinical and literature signals suggest significant repurposing potential in oncology, specifically lung and prostate cancer, as well as metabolic-related inflammatory conditions like psoriasis and NAFLD.
key_findings: Metformin is FDA-approved as an adjunct to diet and exercise for glycemic control in adults and children with type 2 diabetes mellitus.
Active Phase 2 clinical trials (NCT04931017) are investigating metformin for lung cancer chemoprevention and immune system reprogramming in high-risk overweight individuals.
A Phase 4 trial (NCT03629639) is exploring the efficacy of metformin in treating Psoriasis Vulgaris combined with metabolic disorders via gut microbiota modulation.
Literature and clinical signals indicate potential in Non-alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD), though some pilot studies (NCT02457286) have been withdrawn.
Market signals for diabetes-related indications remain high, supported by significant global health expenditure data in various regions.
Mechanistic evidence suggests metformin influences insulin signaling, RNA m6A methylation, and the PI3K/AKT signaling pathway.
top_repurposing_opportunities: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM): Highest intelligence score (26) with strong literature and market alignment.
Lung Carcinoma: Clinical signal score of 20, supported by an active Phase 2 trial for chemoprevention.
Psoriasis Vulgaris with Metabolic Disorders: Phase 4 clinical investigation into comorbid metabolic and inflammatory management.
Metabolic Disorders: Literature-based opportunity (score 22) focusing on insulin signaling and lipid metabolism.
Non-alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD): Investigational use for improving insulin resistance, despite previous trial withdrawals.
risks_and_limitations: Strict regulatory contraindications for patients with renal impairment (eGFR below 30 mL/min/1.73 mÂ²) and acute or chronic metabolic acidosis.
Risk of Metformin-associated lactic acidosis (MALA), especially in patients with hepatic impairment or conditions predisposing to hypoxia.
Common gastrointestinal adverse events including diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal discomfort, which may limit patient adherence.
Potential for Vitamin B12 deficiency leading to complications such as peripheral neuropathy and anemia.
Clinical novelty is reduced for diabetes-related indications as they already appear in approved regulatory texts.
llm_report.final_recommendation: Advance clinical evaluation of Metformin for Lung Carcinoma and Psoriasis with metabolic comorbidities, leveraging its established safety profile. Future trials must incorporate rigorous screening for renal function and monitoring for Vitamin B12 deficiency to mitigate known regulatory and safety risks.
